title: Cotton rag preparation for papermaking
sources: Dard Hunter, Old Papermaking (1923, public domain) — cotton-rag preparation and beating traditions
notes: Preparing cotton rags for pulping: removing synthetic threads, washing out dressing, optional alkali cooking to weaken the fibres for the blender, cutting to small pieces for easier pulping. Covers the difference between cotton-linter (already short-fibre) and torn cotton rags (long-fibre, needs more beating). The papermaking-blender preamble applies.
